Summary: Login error message reveals whether email is registered
Description:
When a user enters an unregistered email on the login form, the error message differs from the one shown for a registered email with a wrong password. An attacker can use this difference to enumerate which email addresses have accounts on the site (user enumeration attack).

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open https://automationexercise.com/login
2. Enter an email that has never been registered (e.g., definitely_not_a_user_99999@example.com)
3. Enter any password
4. Click Login
5. Observe the error message
6. Now repeat with a REGISTERED email but a WRONG password
7. Compare the two messages

Expected: Same generic message for both cases (OWASP best practice).
Actual: Messages differ, leaking which emails are registered.

Reference: OWASP A07:2021 — Identification and Authentication Failures

Summary: Cart contents are lost after logout and login
Description:
When a logged-in user adds products to their cart, logs out, and logs back in, the cart is empty. Expected behavior is that the cart is server-side persisted and restored on next login.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Log in as qa_orkhan@test.com / Test@1234
2. Add 2 products to the cart
3. Verify cart contains both items
4. Click Logout
5. Log back in with the same account
6. Open cart

Expected: Cart still contains the 2 products.
Actual: Cart is empty.

Root cause hypothesis: cart is stored only in browser session and cleared on logout. No server-side persistence keyed by user_id.

Impact: Lost sales; broken cross-device flow; modern e-commerce baseline expectation.

Summary: Login fails when email is pasted with leading/trailing whitespace
Description:
When a user copies their email from another app (password manager, email signature, Slack) and pastes it into the login form, leading or trailing whitespace is preserved. The server treats ' qa@test.com ' as different from 'qa@test.com' and rejects login with the generic 'incorrect' message, leaving the user confused.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. In any text editor, type ' qa_orkhan@test.com ' (with surrounding spaces)
2. Copy the full string including spaces
3. Paste into the email field on the login form
4. Enter the correct password
5. Click Login

Expected: Form trims whitespace on paste or submit; login succeeds.
Actual: Login fails with generic error; user has no clue that invisible spaces are the cause.

Suggested fix: Apply value.trim() on the paste event of the email input. Server should also defensively trim before lookup.

Summary: Add Cypress E2E coverage for checkout flow
Description:
As a QA engineer
I want automated Cypress coverage for the AE checkout flow
So that regressions in payment / address steps are caught before release.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Spec at cypress/e2e/automationexercise/checkout.cy.js
- Page Object for CheckoutPage
- Covers: address review, place order, payment form, confirmation
- Maps to manual test cases TC-CHECKOUT-001..014
- Passes locally and in GitHub Actions CI
